name: threejs-builder
description: Creates simple Three.js web apps with scene setup, lighting, geometries, materials, animations, and responsive rendering. Use for: "Create a threejs scene/app/showcase" or when user wants 3D web content. Supports ES modules, modern Three.js r150+ APIs.
capabilities: Generates complete HTML files with Three.js ES module setup
Creates 3D scenes with geometries, materials, and lighting
Implements animations using setAnimationFrame loop pattern
Adds interactive camera controls and mouse interaction
Builds particle systems and instanced meshes for performance
Supports advanced topics like shaders, post-processing, and physics
author: CesarAugustusGroB
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing Three.js tutorials and code examples. All 140 static findings are false positives. The scanner misidentified JavaScript template literals (backticks) as Ruby/shell backtick execution, GLSL shader code as command execution, and legitimate graphics programming terms (raycaster, loader, copy) as security threats. This skill contains no executable code, no network calls, no file system access, and no security-sensitive operations.
valueStatement: Creating 3D web content requires understanding WebGL, shaders, and 3D graphics programming. This skill generates complete Three.js applications with scene setup, lighting, materials, animations, and responsive rendering using modern ES modules.
limitations: Creates simple applications, not complex games or experiences
Uses CDN imports, requires internet for initial library load
Focuses on basic to intermediate Three.js patterns
No server-side rendering or build tool integration

useCases.title: Create 3D portfolio sites
useCases.description: Build interactive 3D hero sections, product showcases, and animated backgrounds for modern web portfolios.

useCases.title: Prototype 3D interfaces
useCases.description: Quickly visualize and test 3D UI concepts, product configurators, and interactive data visualizations.

useCases.title: Teach 3D web concepts
useCases.description: Generate clean, educational Three.js examples for teaching WebGL, 3D math, and interactive graphics.

promptTemplates.title: Basic 3D Scene
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Three.js application with a rotating colorful cube, proper lighting, and responsive design. Use modern ES modules.

promptTemplates.title: Interactive Product
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Three.js scene showing a 3D product like a watch or shoe with orbit controls, realistic lighting, and smooth materials.

promptTemplates.title: Particle System
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a Three.js app with thousands of animated particles forming interesting patterns with mouse interaction.

promptTemplates.title: Data Visualization
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a 3D bar chart or scatter plot in Three.js with animated data transitions and interactive camera controls.

bestPractices: Always include proper lighting since most materials require it
Import controls and loaders from examples/jsm/ path
Create geometries and materials once, reuse for performance
antiPatterns: Never create new geometries in the animation loop
Avoid legacy CommonJS patterns or global THREE variable
Do not forget to add objects to the scene or they will not render
Never skip the resize event listener for responsive design

faq.question: Which browsers support Three.js?
faq.answer: Any browser with WebGL support including Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge. Three.js falls back to canvas rendering if WebGL is unavailable.

faq.question: Can I use this with React or Vue?
faq.answer: Yes. For React, consider react-three-fiber. For Vue, mount the Three.js canvas in a component mounted hook.

faq.question: How do I add this to my existing website?
faq.answer: Copy the generated HTML or extract the JavaScript. Import Three.js from CDN or npm before your custom code.

faq.question: Is Three.js free for commercial use?
faq.answer: Yes. Three.js uses MIT license. You can freely use it in commercial projects and client work.

faq.question: Why is my scene black?
faq.answer: Most materials need lighting. Add AmbientLight or DirectionalLight, or use MeshBasicMaterial which does not require lights.

faq.question: How can I optimize performance?
faq.answer: Reuse geometries and materials, limit shadow resolution, use InstancedMesh for many objects, and keep triangle counts reasonable.
